message:
fix(doctor): classify DESC index ordering as benign frankensqlite artifact

Frankensqlite's B-tree implementation stores DESC index entries in a
different order than C sqlite3 expects. This causes integrity_check to
report "entries are out of order for their declared key directions" even
on freshly created databases. Previously this was treated as an Error,
which caused doctor --repair to trigger a JSONL rebuild that produced
the exact same "error" — an infinite loop that could never verify clean.

Now "out of order" messages are classified as benign (Warn) alongside
"never used" pages and "missing from index" (both also frankensqlite
artifacts). VACUUM is no longer triggered for this pattern since it
cannot fix it (it recreates the same B-tree layout).

The write probe added in the previous commit still catches actual
corruption that makes writes fail even when integrity_check passes.

Fixes the concurrency test regression from the previous commit.
Part of #245.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
